description: Fixture-backed unsupported and partial-support boundary rows. These rows are negative evidence only; they do not add runtime support.

rows.claimBoundary: Live Search support stays bounded to connect/catalog/_search/render/delete-plan/scoped-error slices. Fixture and live evidence do not promote broader admin, global audit/security dashboards, profile/explain, raw target, or actual delete-by-query execution.

docs.mustContain: Search fixture files are contract evidence and do not promote unwired Search paths to live runtime support.
Search uses an index-catalog-first workbench boundary
Search live HTTP/admin promotion remains owned by the Search roadmap/milestone
Initial Search workbench load is index-catalog-first

rows.minimumRow: MSSQL and Oracle bounded Runtime Happy Path smoke slices
rows.claimBoundary: Issue #907 wires representative Runtime Happy Path smoke for the already-promoted SQL Server and Oracle runtime slices. SQL Server stays bounded to catalog/query/cancel/tabular support plus editRows through frontend SQL batch with primary-key projection, #1071 promotes bounded structured table/index/constraint DDL through the shared StructurePanel dispatch, and #1642 promotes vendor-restorable schema-dump export (T-SQL bracket/INSERT dialect over stream_table_rows), while admin, DB-level import/backup/restore, full T-SQL parity, and full workbench parity remain unsupported. Oracle dissolves the runtime slice and wires the full adapter (#1072): catalog/query/cancel/tabular runtime, key-projected editRows through frontend SQL batch, tested SELECT/DML/DDL Safe Mode classification, bounded editor assistance, the #907 smoke path, and bounded structured table/index/constraint DDL through the shared StructurePanel dispatch, and #1674 vendor-restorable schema-dump export (Oracle double-quote/INSERT dialect over stream_table_rows) are supported, while SID, TNS, wallet, TLS, advanced auth, switch database, raw DDL/admin, DB-level import/backup-restore, full parser/completion promotion, trigger DDL, and PL/SQL body/package authoring work remain unsupported or unclaimed.

docs.mustContain: MSSQL runtime support now covers SQL-auth/TDS connection, catalog browse/schema/indexes/constraints/relationships, query, multi-statement execution, cancellation, tabular result rendering, and editRows through the frontend SQL batch path with primary-key projection.
Bounded structured table/index/constraint DDL is now wired through the shared StructurePanel path (#1071).

Admin/security/jobs/users/roles, DB-level import/backup/restore, profiler/activity dashboards, full T-SQL semantic parity, full workbench parity, and SQLCMD/meta-command/procedure-body scripting remain unsupported.
Oracle supports service-name lifecycle plus a bounded #905/#906 catalog/query/cancel/tabular/edit-row runtime slice.
Bounded structured table/index/constraint DDL is now wired through the shared StructurePanel path (#1072).
MSSQL: runtime catalog/query/edit-row support is active for issue #903.
Oracle: bounded catalog/query/cancel/tabular/edit-row runtime support is active

rows.minimumRow: Valkey full Redis compatibility/collection-write smoke non-claim
rows.claimBoundary: Valkey shares the Redis string plus hash/list/set/zset KvMutationPanel write surface (#1075); full Redis compatibility and Valkey collection-write smoke coverage stay deferred.

docs.mustContain: Full Redis compatibility, CLI/admin parity, cluster/pubsub/modules, and consumer-group flows remain out of scope.

rows.claimBoundary: Redis command editor remains a bounded allowlist. Full CLI parsing, admin, cluster, pubsub, modules, scripting, and consumer-group workflows stay unsupported.

docs.mustContain: Full CLI/admin parity, consumer-group stream UI, cluster/pubsub/modules/consumer-group management

rows.claimBoundary: Mongo support stays on whitelisted document workflows. Arbitrary JavaScript, shell helpers, cursor callbacks, cross-db navigation, server-version promotion, native document-first panels, and full-support parity stay out of scope.

docs.mustContain: Arbitrary JavaScript shell execution, shell helpers, unsupported cursor helpers, multiple statements, cross-db shell navigation, server-version feature promotion gates, native document-first result panels, and full-support parity remain out of scope.
